title: Dense Indian Avocado Sambhar
description: A variation of the traditional South Indian sambhar, this recipe incorporates the richness of avocado pulp. The bitter taste of the avocado is balanced by the earthy flavor of bhuna onions.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add 1 large onion, finely chopped, and sauté until it turns golden brown.
Add 2 cloves of garlic, minced, and sauté for another minute.
Add 1-inch piece of ginger, grated, and sauté for another minute.
Add 1 large tomato, diced, and sauté until it's soft and mushy.
Add 1/2 cup of bhuna onions and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric and 1/2 teaspoon of mustard seeds and sauté for another minute.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of asafoetida and sauté for 30 seconds.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of salt and mix well.
Add 1 cup of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup of coconut milk and mix well.
Add 1 cup of mashed avocado pulp and mix well.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.
